proven.claim: Proven: return value is fully and deterministically bound to whether execSync throws.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; Proven: return value is fully and deterministically bound to whether execSync throws.
; The try/catch has no other branches — the mapping is total and binary.
; Model: exit_code ∈ {0, 1}, func_return ∈ {0, 1}

(declare-const exit_code   Int) ; 0 = execSync succeeds, 1 = execSync throws
(declare-const func_return Int) ; 0 = false, 1 = true

; Code transitions (the two return statements in try/catch)
(assert (=> (= exit_code 0)           (= func_return 1)))
(assert (=> (not (= exit_code 0))     (= func_return 0)))

; Negate the property: func_return is neither 0 nor 1
(assert (and (not (= func_return 0)) (not (= func_return 1))))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — return value is always a proper boolean, no third outcome possible

proven.claim: Proven: ANY exception (not just "not a git repo") causes the function to return false.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; Proven: ANY exception (not just "not a git repo") causes the function to return false.
; ENOENT (bad path), permission denied, git not installed — all map to false.
; This is a proven structural property of the catch-all handler.

(declare-const exception_is_git_related Int) ; 1 = git said "not a repo", 0 = other error
(declare-const exception_occurred      Int) ; 1 = execSync threw
(declare-const func_return             Int) ; 0 = false, 1 = true

; Code transition: the catch block is catch {} — no filtering on exception type
(assert (=> (= exception_occurred 1) (= func_return 0)))

; Attempt to negate: exception occurred but function returned true
(assert (= exception_occurred 1))
(assert (= func_return 1))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — any throw unconditionally yields false

violations.claim: VIOLATION: git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree outputs the string "false" with exit
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5 — Semantic Correctness
; VIOLATION: git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree outputs the string "false" with exit
; code 0 when cwd is inside the .git/ directory (not the work tree). The function
; discards stdout and uses only the exit code, so it returns true when the semantic
; answer is false.
;
; Code transition: function_return is bound to exit_code, NOT to stdout content.

(declare-const exit_code             Int) ; 0 = command exited 0, 1 = threw
(declare-const stdout_is_true        Int) ; 1 = stdout "true", 0 = stdout "false"
(declare-const is_inside_work_tree   Int) ; 1 = semantic ground truth
(declare-const func_return           Int) ; 1 = what the function returns

; Code transition A: function maps exit_code → return (ignores stdout_is_true)
(assert (=> (= exit_code 0)       (= func_return 1)))
(assert (=> (not (= exit_code 0)) (= func_return 0)))

; Code transition B: semantic ground truth requires BOTH exit 0 AND stdout "true"
(assert (=> (and (= exit_code 0) (= stdout_is_true 1)) (= is_inside_work_tree 1)))
(assert (=> (not (and (= exit_code 0) (= stdout_is_true 1))) (= is_inside_work_tree 0)))

; Concrete path: inside .git/ directory — command exits 0, stdout is "false"
(assert (= exit_code 0))
(assert (= stdout_is_true 0))

; Violation: function return diverges from semantic truth
(assert (not (= func_return is_inside_work_tree)))

(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: exit_code=0, stdout_is_true=0 → func_return=1, is_inside_work_tree=0
; The function returns true when run from inside .git/, contradicting the promise.

violations.claim: VIOLATION: The constructor accepts any string for projectRoot with no validation
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3 — Calling Context / Constructor Validation Gap
; VIOLATION: The constructor accepts any string for projectRoot with no validation
; that it is an absolute path. Node.js resolves relative cwd strings against
; process.cwd() inside execSync. The function then checks a different directory
; than the caller intended, and may return true for the wrong location.
;
; Code transition: this.projectRoot is stored as-is; execSync receives it as cwd
; without absolutization (no path.resolve() applied before the call).

(declare-const project_root_is_absolute  Int) ; 1 = absolute, 0 = relative
(declare-const resolved_dir_is_git_repo  Int) ; 1 = process.cwd()+relative is a git repo
(declare-const intended_dir_is_git_repo  Int) ; 1 = the caller's intended dir is a git repo
(declare-const exit_code                 Int) ; 0 = success, 1 = throws
(declare-const func_return               Int) ; 1 = true, 0 = false

; Code transition: execSync uses this.projectRoot directly as cwd
; When relative, the OS resolves it against process.cwd(), not the caller's intent
(assert (=> (= project_root_is_absolute 0) (= exit_code (- 1 resolved_dir_is_git_repo))))
; i.e., exit_code=0 iff resolved_dir_is_git_repo=1

; Code transition: function return is bound to exit_code
(assert (=> (= exit_code 0)       (= func_return 1)))
(assert (=> (not (= exit_code 0)) (= func_return 0)))

; Violation scenario: relative path, resolved dir IS a git repo, intended dir IS NOT
(assert (= project_root_is_absolute 0))
(assert (= resolved_dir_is_git_repo 1))
(assert (= intended_dir_is_git_repo 0))

; Consequence: func_return=1 but intended_dir_is_git_repo=0
(assert (not (= func_return intended_dir_is_git_repo)))

(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: relative projectRoot="." when process.cwd() is inside a git repo,
; but the caller's intended directory is not. Function returns true incorrectly.

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; Proven: return value is fully and deterministically bound to whether execSync throws.
; The try/catch has no other branches — the mapping is total and binary.
; Model: exit_code ∈ {0, 1}, func_return ∈ {0, 1}

(declare-const exit_code   Int) ; 0 = execSync succeeds, 1 = execSync throws
(declare-const func_return Int) ; 0 = false, 1 = true

; Code transitions (the two return statements in try/catch)
(assert (=> (= exit_code 0)           (= func_return 1)))
(assert (=> (not (= exit_code 0))     (= func_return 0)))

; Negate the property: func_return is neither 0 nor 1
(assert (and (not (= func_return 0)) (not (= func_return 1))))

; Expected: unsat — return value is always a proper boolean, no third outcome possible

(assert (= exception_occurred 1))
(assert (= func_return 1))

; PRINCIPLE: P5 — Semantic Correctness
; VIOLATION: git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree outputs the string "false" with exit
; code 0 when cwd is inside the .git/ directory (not the work tree). The function
; discards stdout and uses only the exit code, so it returns true when the semantic
; answer is false.
;
; Code transition: function_return is bound to exit_code, NOT to stdout content.

(declare-const exit_code             Int) ; 0 = command exited 0, 1 = threw
(declare-const stdout_is_true        Int) ; 1 = stdout "true", 0 = stdout "false"
(declare-const is_inside_work_tree   Int) ; 1 = semantic ground truth
(declare-const func_return           Int) ; 1 = what the function returns

; Code transition A: function maps exit_code → return (ignores stdout_is_true)
(assert (=> (= exit_code 0)       (= func_return 1)))
(assert (=> (not (= exit_code 0)) (= func_return 0)))

; Code transition B: semantic ground truth requires BOTH exit 0 AND stdout "true"
(assert (=> (and (= exit_code 0) (= stdout_is_true 1)) (= is_inside_work_tree 1)))
(assert (=> (not (and (= exit_code 0) (= stdout_is_true 1))) (= is_inside_work_tree 0)))

; Concrete path: inside .git/ directory — command exits 0, stdout is "false"
(assert (= exit_code 0))
(assert (= stdout_is_true 0))

; Violation: function return diverges from semantic truth
(assert (not (= func_return is_inside_work_tree)))

(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: exit_code=0, stdout_is_true=0 → func_return=1, is_inside_work_tree=0
; The function returns true when run from inside .git/, contradicting the promise.

; without absolutization (no path.resolve() applied before the call).

(declare-const project_root_is_absolute  Int) ; 1 = absolute, 0 = relative
(declare-const resolved_dir_is_git_repo  Int) ; 1 = process.cwd()+relative is a git repo
(declare-const intended_dir_is_git_repo  Int) ; 1 = the caller's intended dir is a git repo

; Code transition: execSync uses this.projectRoot directly as cwd

; but the caller's intended directory is not. Function returns true incorrectly.

proven.claim: Proven: every file pushed to addedFiles is also in changedFiles.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; Proven: every file pushed to addedFiles is also in changedFiles.
; Transition A: changedFiles.push(file) fires unconditionally once parts.length >= 2.
; Transition B: addedFiles.push(file) fires only when status starts with "A",
;               which requires the same parts.length >= 2 guard to have passed.
; Negation of property: file in addedFiles AND NOT in changedFiles → expect unsat.

(declare-const parts_length Int)
(declare-const status_starts_A Int)
(declare-const in_changed Int)
(declare-const in_added Int)

; Precondition: parts.length guard passes (line has at least 2 tab-separated parts)
(assert (>= parts_length 2))

; Transition A: changedFiles.push runs unconditionally when guard passes
(assert (=> (>= parts_length 2) (= in_changed 1)))

; Transition B: addedFiles.push runs only when status starts with "A"
(assert (=> (= status_starts_A 1) (= in_added 1)))
(assert (=> (= status_starts_A 0) (= in_added 0)))

; Scenario: status IS "A", file is in addedFiles
(assert (= status_starts_A 1))

; Negation: file NOT in changedFiles despite being in addedFiles
(assert (= in_changed 0))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — changedFiles.push always precedes addedFiles.push in the same guard scope

proven.claim: Proven: if execSync throws for any reason, parseDiff returns the initial
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; Proven: if execSync throws for any reason, parseDiff returns the initial
; empty result — all four arrays have zero elements.
; The bare `catch { return result }` catches all exception types.

(declare-const execsync_throws Int)
(declare-const changed_count Int)
(declare-const added_count Int)
(declare-const deleted_count Int)
(declare-const modified_count Int)

; Transition: catch block executes — returns result as initialized (all counts = 0)
(assert (=> (= execsync_throws 1) (= changed_count 0)))
(assert (=> (= execsync_throws 1) (= added_count 0)))
(assert (=> (= execsync_throws 1) (= deleted_count 0)))
(assert (=> (= execsync_throws 1) (= modified_count 0)))

; Scenario: execSync throws
(assert (= execsync_throws 1))

; Negation: any array is non-empty despite exception
(assert (or (> changed_count 0)
            (> added_count 0)
            (> deleted_count 0)
            (> modified_count 0)))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — the bare catch returns before any push can occur

proven.claim: Proven: if output is the empty string, the early return fires before
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; Proven: if output is the empty string, the early return fires before
; the parsing loop, leaving all arrays at their initialized empty state.

(declare-const output_length Int)
(declare-const changed_count Int)
(declare-const added_count Int)

; Transition: `if (!output) return result` — empty string is falsy in JS/TS
(assert (=> (= output_length 0) (= changed_count 0)))
(assert (=> (= output_length 0) (= added_count 0)))

; Scenario: output is empty
(assert (= output_length 0))

; Negation: arrays non-empty despite empty output
(assert (or (> changed_count 0) (> added_count 0)))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — early return precedes the split/loop entirely

violations.claim: git diff --name-status emits rename lines as: R100\told.ts\tnew.ts
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; git diff --name-status emits rename lines as: R100\told.ts\tnew.ts
; Code computes: file = parts[parts.length - 1]
; When parts.length = 3, index_used = 2 (destination). parts[1] (source) is
; never referenced again and is never pushed to any array.
; A file that existed before the rename vanishes from all output.

(declare-const parts_length Int)
(declare-const index_of_file_used Int)
(declare-const index_of_source Int)
(declare-const source_in_changed Int)
(declare-const source_in_deleted Int)

; Transition: rename line produces 3 tab-separated parts
(assert (= parts_length 3))

; Transition: code computes parts[parts.length - 1] → index 2 (destination)
(assert (= index_of_file_used (- parts_length 1)))

; Transition: source lives at index 1; it is a different index than the one used
(assert (= index_of_source 1))
(assert (not (= index_of_file_used index_of_source)))

; Transition: only the destination (index_of_file_used = 2) is pushed to arrays
; Source (index 1) is never pushed to changedFiles
(assert (= source_in_changed 0))
; The R-status branch pushes only `file` (dest) to modifiedFiles — not source to deletedFiles
(assert (= source_in_deleted 0))

; Violation: source file appears in git output but is absent from all result arrays
(assert (= source_in_changed 0))
(assert (= source_in_deleted 0))

(check-sat)
; sat → renamed source is invisible to callers; deletedFiles never records it
; Witness: parts = ["R100", "old.ts", "new.ts"], index_used=2, source never pushed

violations.claim: git diff emits status codes beyond A/D/M/R: C (copy), T (type-change),
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; git diff emits status codes beyond A/D/M/R: C (copy), T (type-change),
; U (unmerged), X (unknown). parseDiff has no branch for these codes.
; Such a file is pushed to changedFiles but not to any specific array,
; violating the structural invariant that changedFiles = union of specific arrays.

(declare-const parts_length Int)
(declare-const status_starts_A Int)
(declare-const status_starts_D Int)
(declare-const status_starts_M Int)
(declare-const status_starts_R Int)
(declare-const in_changed Int)
(declare-const in_specific Int)

; Transition: parts.length = 2 → guard passes, loop body executes
(assert (= parts_length 2))
(assert (>= parts_length 2))

; Transition: status is "C" (copy) — does not start with A, D, M, or R
(assert (= status_starts_A 0))
(assert (= status_starts_D 0))
(assert (= status_starts_M 0))
(assert (= status_starts_R 0))

; Transition: changedFiles.push fires unconditionally
(assert (= in_changed 1))

; Transition: all four if/else branches fail — no specific-array push occurs
(assert (= in_specific 0))

; Violation: changedFiles contains the file; no specific array does
(assert (> in_changed in_specific))

(check-sat)
; sat → a copied file (status C) appears only in changedFiles, never in modifiedFiles/addedFiles
; Callers iterating addedFiles + modifiedFiles + deletedFiles will miss it entirely

; PRINCIPLE: P5
; Contract: "throw on malformed input"
; Actual: lines with parts.length < 2 hit `continue` — they are silently dropped.
; execSync failures hit `catch { return result }` — exception is swallowed.
; Neither path throws. The function can never satisfy the "or throw" branch of its contract.

(declare-const malformed_line_count Int)
(declare-const execsync_error_count Int)
(declare-const throws_emitted Int)

; Transition: malformed lines (< 2 parts) are processed by `continue`, not throw
(assert (>= malformed_line_count 1))
(assert (=> (>= malformed_line_count 1) (= throws_emitted 0)))

; Transition: execSync errors are caught and swallowed — empty result returned
(assert (>= execsync_error_count 0))
(assert (=> (>= execsync_error_count 1) (= throws_emitted 0)))

; Scenario: malformed input exists
(assert (= malformed_line_count 3))

; Violation: the function has no code path that emits a throw
(assert (= throws_emitted 0))

(check-sat)
; sat → the contract promise "throw on malformed input" is structurally unreachable;
; callers receive a partial or empty DiffResult with no indication of the malformation
